candidate_problems.text: Identify specific institutional error-correction mechanisms that predict long-run safety gains independently of baseline wealth, demographics, incarceration policy, and national crime trends.

Determine whether cities can become safer through exogenous structural changes alone, without improved local learning institutions, which would limit the conjecture's claimed primacy of epistemic capacity.
Explain why some cities with strong measurement and pilot programs still fail to reduce violence, if error-correcting institutions are the decisive difference.
